title: abrus parvifolius
intro: Abrus parvifolius, also known as the small-leaved crab's eye or the Jequirity bean, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its seeds and leaves. The seeds of Abrus parvifolius contain a toxic compound called abrin, but in small amounts, they have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, providing relief from pain and inflammation and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the seeds and leaves of Abrus parvifolius are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness their medicinal properties.
